chunks.text: Jan M. Rabaey is Professor Emeritus at UC Berkeley with contact information including 563 Cory Hall, phone 510-6422328, and email jan_rabaey@berkeley.edu. He holds the Donald O. Pederson Distinguished Professorship and is a founding director of the Berkeley Wireless Research Center (BWRC) and Berkeley Ubiquitous SwarmLab, having served twice as Electrical Engineering Division Chair. His high-impact contributions span advanced wireless systems, low power integrated circuits, sensor networks, and ubiquitous computing. Current interests focus on next-generation integrated wireless systems and cyber-biological interactions.
chunks.question: What is Jan Rabaey's current academic position?

chunks.text: Jan Rabaey has received major awards including the IEEE Mac Van Valkenburg Award, EDAA Lifetime Achievement award, and SIA University Researcher Award. He is an IEEE Fellow, a member of the Royal Flemish Academy of Sciences and Arts of Belgium, and holds honorary doctorates from Lund, Antwerp, and Tampere. He has been involved in various start-up ventures. His research areas include Information, Data, Network, and Communication Sciences; Design, Modeling and Analysis; Power and Energy; Integrated Circuits; Signal Processing; and Computer architecture.
chunks.question: Which prestigious academy is Jan Rabaey a member of?
chunks.answer: Royal Flemish Academy of Sciences and Arts.

chunks.text: Research centers associated with Jan Rabaey include the Berkeley Wireless Research Center (BWRC), Center for Neural Engineering & Prostheses (CNEP), and CITRIS People and Robots (CPAR). Selected publications highlight his work in wireless systems and low-power design, such as 'Wireless recording in the peripheral nervous system with ultrasonic neural dust' (Neuron, 2016) and 'An ultra-low-power injection locked transmitter for wireless sensor networks' (IEEE J. Solid-State Circuits, 2006). Other notable works include 'ZUMA: A platform for smart-home environments' (2006) and 'Energy Scavenging for Wireless Sensor Networks' (2003).
chunks.question: What is the title of Rabaey's 2016 publication on neural recording?
chunks.answer: Wireless recording with ultrasonic neural dust.

chunks.text: Additional publications by Jan Rabaey include 'A power-managed protocol processor for wireless sensor networks' (2006), 'Overcoming untuned radios in wireless networks with network coding' (IEEE Trans. Information Theory, 2006), and 'A 89-µW 1.9-GHz CMOS oscillator using FBAR resonator' (2005). He co-edited 'Ambient Intelligence' (2005) and authored 'Digital Integrated Circuits: A Design Perspective' (2003). Earlier works include 'PicoRadios for wireless sensor networks' (2002) and 'Reconfigurable processing: The solution to low-power programmable DSP' (1997), demonstrating a long-term focus on low-power and wireless technologies.
chunks.question: What book did Rabaey co-edit in 2005?

chunks.text: Selected honors and awards received by Jan Rabaey include the IEEE Donald O. Pederson Award in Solid-State Circuits (2025), IEEE CASS John Choma Education Award (2025), IEEE James H. Mulligan Jr. Education Medal (2024), SRC Aristotle Award (2017), and election to the Royal Flemish Academy of Belgium for Science and the Arts (2011). Other accolades are the SIA University Research Award (2010), EDAA Lifetime Achievement Award (2009), IEEE CASS Mac E. Van Valkenburg Award (2008), and IEEE Fellow (1995), highlighting his significant contributions to electrical engineering and computer sciences.
chunks.question: Which award did Rabaey receive in 2024?
chunks.answer: IEEE James H. Mulligan Jr. Education Medal.
